title: A machine learning bioinformatics method to predict biological activity from biosynthetic gene clusters
research_objectives: Develop machine learning method to predict natural product bioactivity from BGC sequences
Address bioactivity prioritization bottleneck in drug discovery
Enable prediction of desired biological functions (e.g., antibiotic activity) without requiring production and assaying
Increase efficiency of natural product discovery pipeline

problem_statement.challenge: Over 147,000 BGC sequences identified by antiSMASH but prioritization remains difficult
problem_statement.current_limitation: Cannot predict activity of natural products from BGC sequences using traditional methods
problem_statement.discovery_bottleneck: Production, purification, and assaying of compounds is major bottleneck in drug discovery
problem_statement.existing_gap: Many structurally fascinating molecules lack identified functions

research_scope.focus: Bioactivity prediction from BGC genomic information
research_scope.goal: Enable prioritization of BGCs based on predicted function before production

existing_tools_analysis.general_limitation: Existing tools cannot broadly predict natural product functions
existing_tools_analysis.knowledge_gap: Majority of structurally complex molecules have unknown bioactivity

drug_discovery_context.historical_productivity: Natural products historically rich source of drugs
drug_discovery_context.current_challenge: Traditional bioactivity-guided screening rediscovers known compounds

bgc_identification_landscape.status: Identification tools mature but bioactivity prediction lacking

research_innovation.hypothesis: BGC genomic features encode bioactivity information
research_innovation.approach: Machine learning to extract predictive features from BGC sequences
research_innovation.validation: Compare predictions to experimental bioactivity data
